reviews_data.review_text: After coming here for a year, I can still say that I really like this little downtown spot. I don't go for the food because of my own dietary restrictions but everything looks great. I think the atmosphere is wonderful, inclusive, and just downright 'Gastonia'. The owner is really nice once you get to know him. The bar staff are always on point. I have never had a dirty wine glass, the prices are quite reasonable for all drinks, and the place is super clean. Wednesday is half price house wine bottles and I love the Dona Paula Red Blend. They do have an array of events such as wine dinners, local vendors that come in on the weekends, and different tasting events. I think this is exactly what Gastonia needs to get people out and about downtown. I'm really glad this place is here and look forward to another year of frequenting our local downtown spot.
Wonderful drinks and great service!
I wish businesses would update their info to win. They're open, drove all the way here and it's not even open. Not even a sign on the door saying what time they do open.
Great place! Thank You.
My friend and I love coming here on Wednesdays! They have 1/2 off bottles of wine and Carson is AMAZING! She always remembers are orders and takes great care of us!

To be honest, we went because there was a little Wiccan fair inside, where several people were selling their super cool, mostly homemade items. There was lots of jewelry, candles, tarot decks, and so much more. They were also offering tarot readings.

As far as the Pour House itself, though we didn't order any food or drink, I can comment on other things. There were plenty of comfy, cozy places to sit, including several patio tables and bar seats. The staff was friendly, the bathrooms clean, and there were several regulars eating and drinking, which is always a good sign!

Though the menu is relatively small, it's locally-sourced and very unique. I'm really looking forward to going back and trying to food and a pint of local brew!

VERY limited parking. There aren't many tables for large groups, however, it is a comfortable open concept atmosphere for hanging out, playing pool, and getting in some drinks and finger foods. Curiously I asked a couple who were already seated what they thought of the food. They seemed to enjoy their meals and said that the food was fresh. In a search for an upbeat scene for casual breakfast, I saw a photo of chicken and waffles in one of the past reviews and was amped up to order it. Unfortunately, this is not on their current menu. I didn't order any food on this trip, but I wouldn't count this place out for other people. The cashier was polite and hospitable. I've posted some pictures of the most current menu. 3 stars due to my limited food experience here.

reviews_data.owner_answer:
Hi Charlie,
I’m not sure which bourbon selection you chose during your most recent visit, however; our team has been trained to guide guests on higher end products to ensure guests understand pricing as they choose higher end products. 
We have very different allocations here in NC than the Mid & West coast. LV is able to provide a very different inventory of high end products.  We try our best to provide high quality at an appropriate price. When it comes to allocation rarities, especially in bourbons,  you may very well see a price increase over other establishments depending on where you’re enjoying throughout the US. 

We appreciate the feedback, and will consider it when pricing products.  

We hope you will continue to support our business and all other small businesses.

reviews_data.review_text: My friend and I were visiting from out of town. We had done a morning hike nearby and searched for local places to eat lunch. We found Gaston Pour House and loved that it was a small place that used local ingredients. They only had the brunch menu available when we got there, which was fine, and both of us really enjoyed our food. The staff really cared about quality and presentation. I had my 1.5yr old daughter with us and since they didn’t have any high chairs she was running around like crazy. Luckily we were the only ones in at that time and the staff was very gracious and understanding about the situation. My friend and I each got a latte on our way out for our drives home and both were excellent. I highly recommend the Tiramisu latte!!!! Definitely give this place a try!

reviews_data.owner_answer:
Melody,
I appreciate you supporting small businesses and your attempt to dine with us. I am  sorry for the less than excellent communication and over all inconvenience you experienced.
We strive to be highly hospitable and our policy as well as our overall expectation is for our team to be welcoming to all guests.
We open at 11:30am Monday- Saturday.
Unfortunately, yet innocently, the evening team may forget to turn the sign to “Closed” and the morning team won’t realize this upon arrival.
I’m so sorry the sign was misleading and I am truly sorry that the communication wasn’t delivered and/or perceived properly.

I can assure you I have addressed and investigated the situation to the best of my ability. We have a zero tolerance policy in regards to discrimination.
The team member on duty is absolutely devastated that the situation was perceived as discriminatory in any way.

We would love a second chance with you and to make you feel welcomed while providing a great experience and great food/beverage.
Thank you for the review. The opportunity to correct situations and overall better our service is top priority.
If you have any other questions, concerns, or would like to further discuss the situation please reach out to me directly.
Chelsea Kern (owner)
GPH@gastonpourhouse.com
reviews_data.review_text: Went to this coffee shop Monday with a few friends. It was 11:05 when we arrived. The sign on the door said we’re open! Come on in! The lady who was working decided to tell us we would be better off going somewhere else or waiting until 11:30 because she wasn’t finished putting the chairs down inside or outside. No other customers were around. I felt as if we were being discriminated against. What small business turns away 3 customers all because the chairs weren’t put down in time? If it were me I would have said come on in let me put the chairs down real fast. Especially since the sign on the door said we’re open! Horrible customer service.

reviews_data.review_text: I had the pleasure of co-hosting a bridal shower at Gaston Pour House over the weekend.  After scouring over so many establishments in the area and not finding the right fit, I got a call back from Chelsea and I knew right off she was the one for the job--I could tell she had her act together and knew I could trust her.  She handled literally everything, making suggestions when needed but never pushy, and the space was perfect for the shower.  Plenty of space and tables to eat and drink plus couches and comfy chairs for gift-opening and chatting.  We did a brunch with mimosas, chicken and waffles, strata, fruit, and spinach dip.  The strata, which I'd never had before, was especially delicious.   Chelsea was a great hostess, making herself available when needed but largely just letting us celebrate the bride to be.  Everyone loved it, there was plenty of food to go around, the cost was very reasonable, but most of all, it was the least stressful party I've ever hosted--I actually got to enjoy it myself!  I highly recommend Chelsea for any special events or catering

Amazing cocktails!
I tried the Bacon Fat Washed Bourbon cocktail (Hogwash) as well as the Mezcal Blackberry(Black Beauty) this evening. Service was fabulous and the cocktails were hand crafted to perfection! So unique

Food and beer was good.   Service was absolutely awful!  Never going back

An honest review,
I have expectations walking into any bar, as I suspect we all do. With this being said I was sorely disappointed with the three tries I gave this place. The atmosphere is cool and that's why it deserves one star. The bartenders are not good whatsoever. The drinks are never made properly nor do they taste pleasant. (Although Douge does make a wonderful old fashioned) the food is very over priced and so are the drinks for the quality that you get. I hope this place improves but until then I would suggest avoiding such a let down.

Terrible attitude from the bartender. We just wanted two coffees to go and were talked to with a condescending tone from the girl with the shaved head. The places aesthetic is nice, but the service is horrid. Thanks for the half filled cup of overpriced coffee.

Used to really like this place when it was zoes so wife and I walked in here to get a drink before getting dinner next door, place nearly empty but got the side eye from the 4 or 5 hipsters that were hanging out at the bar. We were out celebrating our anniversary so I ordered a couple pours of a top shelf bourbon and was GROSSLY overcharged for it!!! A full bottle of barrel bourbon is $80 dollars and I was charged $47 dollars for 2 measly pours, would've refused to pay so much but didn't want to be embarrassed. Felt totally ripped off...Afterwards we weren't checked on or attended to in any way, went next door and got some real service. We won't return.

reviews_data.review_text: Nice atmosphere. Good food. Lazy service. Goat cheese salad and grilled cheese were both delish. Salad came on a weird metal flat pizza plate with paper on it under the salad and none of the greens were cut. We got only a butter knife to cut it- but the paper ripped when you tried to cut the salad greens so there was paper cut up in the salad. A bit of an ordeal but delicious. Bar stools and chairs were wobbly all felt like they were going to collapse. Food was inexpensive and delicious. Fun couches and pool table. 2 of the 3 workers didn’t have masks on 12/12/20. You’re supposed to Go up to the bar to order- we waited there at the “order” cash register as the only non served customers for probably 5-10 minutes (and yes we asked for help) while the bartender washed cups 1 foot away and complained about how she was supposed to be gone already but continued to serve other people. All the while the other employee without a mask tried to work a coffee machine and make small talk. Weird vibes TBH. All around High risk, *potential* high reward. Also high risk for no mask zone if you’re into that.

reviews_data.review_text: After coming here for a year, I can still say that I really like this little downtown spot. I don't go for the food because of my own dietary restrictions but everything looks great. I think the atmosphere is wonderful, inclusive, and just downright 'Gastonia'. The owner is really nice once you get to know him. The bar staff are always on point. I have never had a dirty wine glass, the prices are quite reasonable for all drinks, and the place is super clean. Wednesday is half price house wine bottles and I love the Dona Paula Red Blend. They do have an array of events such as wine dinners, local vendors that come in on the weekends, and different tasting events. I think this is exactly what Gastonia needs to get people out and about downtown. I'm really glad this place is here and look forward to another year of frequenting our local downtown spot.

Hi Charlie,
I’m not sure which bourbon selection you chose during your most recent visit, however; our team has been trained to guide guests on higher end products to ensure guests understand pricing as they choose higher end products. 
We have very different allocations here in NC than the Mid & West coast. LV is able to provide a very different inventory of high end products.  We try our best to provide high quality at an appropriate price. When it comes to allocation rarities, especially in bourbons,  you may very well see a price increase over other establishments depending on where you’re enjoying throughout the US.

Melody,
I appreciate you supporting small businesses and your attempt to dine with us. I am  sorry for the less than excellent communication and over all inconvenience you experienced.
We strive to be highly hospitable and our policy as well as our overall expectation is for our team to be welcoming to all guests.
We open at 11:30am Monday- Saturday.
Unfortunately, yet innocently, the evening team may forget to turn the sign to “Closed” and the morning team won’t realize this upon arrival.
I’m so sorry the sign was misleading and I am truly sorry that the communication wasn’t delivered and/or perceived properly.

I can assure you I have addressed and investigated the situation to the best of my ability. We have a zero tolerance policy in regards to discrimination.
The team member on duty is absolutely devastated that the situation was perceived as discriminatory in any way.

We would love a second chance with you and to make you feel welcomed while providing a great experience and great food/beverage.
Thank you for the review. The opportunity to correct situations and overall better our service is top priority.
If you have any other questions, concerns, or would like to further discuss the situation please reach out to me directly.
Chelsea Kern (owner)
GPH@gastonpourhouse.com

reviews_data.review_text: I had the pleasure of co-hosting a bridal shower at Gaston Pour House over the weekend.  After scouring over so many establishments in the area and not finding the right fit, I got a call back from Chelsea and I knew right off she was the one for the job--I could tell she had her act together and knew I could trust her.  She handled literally everything, making suggestions when needed but never pushy, and the space was perfect for the shower.  Plenty of space and tables to eat and drink plus couches and comfy chairs for gift-opening and chatting.  We did a brunch with mimosas, chicken and waffles, strata, fruit, and spinach dip.  The strata, which I'd never had before, was especially delicious.   Chelsea was a great hostess, making herself available when needed but largely just letting us celebrate the bride to be.  Everyone loved it, there was plenty of food to go around, the cost was very reasonable, but most of all, it was the least stressful party I've ever hosted--I actually got to enjoy it myself!  I highly recommend Chelsea for any special events or catering

Amazing cocktails!
I tried the Bacon Fat Washed Bourbon cocktail (Hogwash) as well as the Mezcal Blackberry(Black Beauty) this evening. Service was fabulous and the cocktails were hand crafted to perfection! So unique
Definitely a gem in Gastonia, excited to go back!!

Really small. Not too much happening. If you're looking for a place to just drink and play a game of pool, it would suffice. Only 1 employee in the whole place, bless his heart. I wasn't impressed but gave it a 3 star rating because the employee at the bar went way above and beyond although he was working alone.
Food and beer was good.   Service was absolutely awful!  Never going back

Nice place, great balance.  Not too uppity, down to earth, very comfortable and relaxing.  Add some white string lights to the back wall (bar side), and it would be perfect warm mood lighting.

An honest review,
I have expectations walking into any bar, as I suspect we all do. With this being said I was sorely disappointed with the three tries I gave this place. The atmosphere is cool and that's why it deserves one star. The bartenders are not good whatsoever. The drinks are never made properly nor do they taste pleasant. (Although Douge does make a wonderful old fashioned) the food is very over priced and so are the drinks for the quality that you get. I hope this place improves but until then I would suggest avoiding such a let down.

Terrible attitude from the bartender. We just wanted two coffees to go and were talked to with a condescending tone from the girl with the shaved head. The places aesthetic is nice, but the service is horrid. Thanks for the half filled cup of overpriced coffee.

Beautiful experience. Fantastic service and staff!

Used to really like this place when it was zoes so wife and I walked in here to get a drink before getting dinner next door, place nearly empty but got the side eye from the 4 or 5 hipsters that were hanging out at the bar. We were out celebrating our anniversary so I ordered a couple pours of a top shelf bourbon and was GROSSLY overcharged for it!!! A full bottle of barrel bourbon is $80 dollars and I was charged $47 dollars for 2 measly pours, would've refused to pay so much but didn't want to be embarrassed. Felt totally ripped off...Afterwards we weren't checked on or attended to in any way, went next door and got some real service. We won't return.

reviews_data.review_text: Nice atmosphere. Good food. Lazy service. Goat cheese salad and grilled cheese were both delish. Salad came on a weird metal flat pizza plate with paper on it under the salad and none of the greens were cut. We got only a butter knife to cut it- but the paper ripped when you tried to cut the salad greens so there was paper cut up in the salad. A bit of an ordeal but delicious. Bar stools and chairs were wobbly all felt like they were going to collapse. Food was inexpensive and delicious. Fun couches and pool table. 2 of the 3 workers didn’t have masks on 12/12/20. You’re supposed to Go up to the bar to order- we waited there at the “order” cash register as the only non served customers for probably 5-10 minutes (and yes we asked for help) while the bartender washed cups 1 foot away and complained about how she was supposed to be gone already but continued to serve other people. All the while the other employee without a mask tried to work a coffee machine and make small talk. Weird vibes TBH. All around High risk, *potential* high reward. Also high risk for no mask zone if you’re into that.
